codeReview.summary: NightDetailView composes the expected W1 UI pieces and state handling, but it fails an explicit expected behavior: baseline bands for intraday glucose/heart-rate strips are not available from the default night-view fetch path.

issues.description: Baseline overlays are looked up by intraday strip metric key (`baselineMap[metricType]`), but the hook call (`useNightView(date)` at line 59) does not request intraday metrics. The night endpoint default baseline set is daily metrics (e.g., rhr/hrv; see `DEFAULT_NIGHT_METRICS` in `src/app/api/views/night/route.ts`), so baseline entries for `glucose`/`heart_rate` are absent in normal usage. This violates the feature expectation that baseline bands are visible on glucose and HR panels.

Tests mock baselines keyed to intraday series metrics (`glucose`, `heart_rate`), which masks the real API-default contract mismatch and allows the baseline-band requirement to appear satisfied in unit tests while failing in integration behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill does not call out that browser-side API calls depend on `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` matching the actual dev server URL/port, which can invalidate manual UI verification even when endpoints are healthy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-04-05T08-36-29-271Z__night-detail-view-page__4a2f3c46-621d-4633-9bf5-45e7c3dbc14e.json` reports browser failure from `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000` while services run on 3100; `src/lib/api-client.ts:22` builds requests from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`; `.factory/services.yaml` defines web on port 3100; handoff.skillFeedback suggests adding this gotcha to the skill.

summary: Reviewed worker transcript skeleton and commit b6b8583. The feature is close, but baseline-band behavior required for glucose/HR strips is not satisfied under the real default night-view API contract, so this review fails with one blocking issue.
